meta.title: Leo Gonsalves — Software engineer
meta.description: Engineer working in React and C# across clinical trials, geospatial tooling, and Belgian payroll. Available for contract and consulting work.

headlines: Programmer with design instincts — drawn to domains that fight back.
Half a decade of React and C# spent in systems with too many moving parts.

about.title: Programmer with design instincts. Drawn to the messy ones.
bodies:
Half a decade spent cooking React and C# code, with a soft spot for slightly absurd problem statements. So far that's meant clinical-trial supply chains, satellite carbon analysis, and Belgian payroll.
Outside work, I build games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. They all serve the same purpose: keeping a beginner's mind near the tools I use professionally.

I'm a programmer with a designer's habits, working mostly in React and C#. Half a decade in, my taste runs toward systems where “this should be simple” turns out to be the most expensive sentence in the room — clinical trials, carbon registries, Belgian payroll.
Outside hours go to games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. The variety keeps a beginner's mind around tools I use professionally.

Half a decade of React and C# spent inside slightly absurd domains — clinical-trial supply, satellite carbon analysis, continental payroll. I'm not picky about the stack so much as the shape of the problem: dense, under-specified, occasionally regulated into a corner.
Outside work I build games, interactive media, tools, and LLM-based experiments. A finished project is the cleanest proof I know that I still enjoy this.

titles: Three companies. Three problem statements that didn't fit on a slide.
Three companies, three regulated industries, one stubborn engineer.
work.lede: Shipped under audit, under deadline, and occasionally under satellite passes.

nside.contribution: Founding-team engineer on Lighthouse — a clinical-trial supply-planning platform modernizing a legacy Java stack.

tris.contribution: Bootstrapped and built a geospatial analysis tool opening carbon-credit access to Brazilian farmers and landowners.

payflip.contribution: Currently building unified card-payment infrastructure to revolutionize Belgian cafeteria-plan benefits and promote salary transparency.

side.title: Smaller things, built for the joy of finishing them.
side.lede: Outside work, mostly weekends. A different register — same instinct.

recolonizer.title: Recolonizer
recolonizer.blurb: A tower defense built solo in Unity — pathing, waves, and a stubborn fondness for C#.

glutton.title: Glutton for Gluten
glutton.blurb: Game-jam platformer; built in a weekend and still walks.

cowboy_investor.title: Cowboy Investor
cowboy_investor.blurb: A small LLM-backed wrapper that pretends to give investment advice.

contact.title: Have a system that's grown teeth?
contact.body: Taking on contract and consulting work — React and C# preferred, ambiguous problem statements welcome.
